description: Document the architecture thesis that recurring billing truth flows from cadence owner to service periods to due invoice windows to persisted invoice detail metadata
Document an explicit system-surface matrix covering runtime billing, invoice generation, credits, APIs, UI, portal, exports, reporting, migrations, and cleanup
Inventory every recurring timing control currently in use, including billing frequency, client anchors, assignment dates, billing_timing, enable_proration, and billing_cycle_alignment
Inventory every persisted date/period field that participates in recurring billing or billed-through semantics across invoices, invoice details, and client billing cycles
Inventory every consumer of service period metadata, including exports, portal views, reporting, credits, and invoice detail readers
Explicitly mark time, usage, materials, and manual-only non-recurring flows as outside the first hard cut while documenting their compatibility boundaries
Build a recurring-billing parity matrix spanning frequency, timing mode, start/end boundaries, pricing schedules, discounts, credits, and charge families
Build parity harness infrastructure that can compare legacy and service-period-first outputs for client-cadence recurring scenarios before cutover
Define cutover-blocking parity regressions separately from acceptable metadata-shape changes so rollout decisions are deterministic
Define representative recurring fixture builders that cover monthly, quarterly, semi-annual, annual, advance, arrears, mid-period starts, mid-period ends, and mixed overlays
Define a canonical service-period data structure with explicit start, end, cadence owner, source obligation, and timing metadata
Define a canonical invoice-window or due-window data structure separate from service periods
Define cadence-owner semantics and lifecycle defaults for recurring obligations
Define a cadence-boundary generator interface with pluggable client-cadence and contract-cadence implementations
Define activity-window intersection rules that combine generated service periods with assignment start and end dates
Define partial-period settlement rules that replace duplicated proration math with one recurring coverage model
Define due-position rules that map service periods to invoice windows for advance and arrears timing
Define one inclusive/exclusive date semantic model across client billing cycles, service periods, assignment dates, and invoice detail persistence
Define a shared persistence contract for invoice detail rows produced from canonical recurring service periods
Add shared recurring timing test utilities and factories that can assert canonical service-period behavior independently of invoice creation
Implement monthly client-cadence service-period boundary generation using current client anchor-day behavior
Implement quarterly client-cadence service-period boundary generation using current anchor month/day behavior
Implement semi-annual client-cadence service-period boundary generation using current anchor month/day behavior
Implement annual client-cadence service-period boundary generation using current anchor month/day behavior
Implement weekly client-cadence service-period boundary generation with explicit [start, end) semantics
Implement bi-weekly client-cadence service-period boundary generation with explicit [start, end) semantics
Preserve current transition-period behavior when client billing anchors change on future non-invoiced cycles
Preserve current [start, end) client billing cycle semantics when translating billing cycles into canonical service periods
Define deterministic client-cadence service-period behavior when historical billing cycles exist but client billing settings changed later
Define safe fallback behavior when legacy or partial client billing cycle data is encountered during service-period generation
Map advance timing to the current service period on the current invoice window without separate service-period logic
Map arrears timing to the previous service period on the current invoice window without separate service-period logic
Replace mid-period start handling with activity-window intersection against generated service periods
Replace mid-period end handling with activity-window intersection against generated service periods
Replace fixed-charge proration factor logic with partial-period settlement derived from actual service-period coverage
Replace product and license proration factor logic with the same partial-period settlement model
Define first-period behavior for newly activated recurring lines under both advance and arrears timing
Define final-period behavior for terminated recurring lines under both advance and arrears timing
Define no-coverage and zero-length behavior so unsupported or empty service periods never create duplicate or spurious recurring charges
Centralize recurring timing resolution so fixed, product, and license charge families no longer implement their own date math
Refactor fixed recurring charge entry points in BillingEngine to consume canonical service periods
Remove direct dependency on resolveServicePeriod for fixed recurring charges
Remove arrears-specific skip branches in fixed recurring billing once service-period eligibility exists
Replace advance termination credit special casing with canonical partial-period settlement for fixed recurring charges
Preserve FMV allocation behavior while changing only the timing source for fixed recurring charges
Preserve pricing-schedule override precedence for fixed recurring charges under service-period-first timing
Preserve contract-level custom rate behavior for fixed recurring lines under service-period-first timing
Preserve tax allocation and tax-region behavior for fixed recurring charges while timing semantics change
Preserve purchase-order and assignment metadata behavior for fixed recurring charges after service-period-first cutover
Persist fixed recurring invoice detail rows with canonical service-period metadata and parity-check them against legacy behavior
Define how bucket or allowance periods map onto canonical recurring service periods for recurring bucket contract lines
Align remaining-bucket and rollover calculations with canonical service-period boundaries where recurring timing currently matters
Preserve bucket overage charging behavior when bucket plans move onto service-period-first timing semantics
Preserve discount applicability evaluation when recurring line timing is driven by canonical service periods
Preserve pricing schedule effective-date evaluation when service periods, not ad hoc billing windows, drive recurring charges
Preserve tax date-selection semantics when recurring charges are produced from canonical service periods
Define behavior for negative recurring totals and credit-generating service allocations under canonical service periods
Ensure prepayment and prepaid-invoice flows consume canonical recurring service-period metadata where they currently depend on recurring timing
Ensure purchase-order support and PO banners continue to interpret recurring timing correctly after service-period cutover
Explicitly document which bucket, time, and usage behaviors remain outside the first recurring service-period implementation
Identify recurring product charge paths that currently rely on late-stage proration adjustment
Refactor recurring product charges to derive timing entirely from canonical service periods
Preserve catalog price lookup versus contract override precedence for recurring product charges under the new timing model
Preserve recurring product tax behavior while moving products onto canonical service periods
Identify recurring license charge paths that currently rely on late-stage proration adjustment
Refactor recurring license charges to derive timing entirely from canonical service periods
Preserve recurring license quantity, price, and renewal-related behavior while timing semantics change
Preserve recurring license tax behavior while moving licenses onto canonical service periods
Persist product invoice detail rows with canonical service-period metadata
Persist license invoice detail rows with canonical service-period metadata
Refactor invoice generation to select due recurring service periods for a billing cycle window instead of deriving recurring service periods inside each charge path
Preserve invoice header billing_period_start and billing_period_end semantics while recurring line details adopt canonical service periods
Preserve createInvoiceFromBillingResult and downstream invoice finalization behavior when recurring charges are service-period-first
Update recurring billing run actions and automatic invoice generation flows to use the new recurring period selection model
Update preview and generate-one-cycle actions to compare and display recurring charges sourced from canonical service periods
Ensure manual invoice creation and editing flows continue to coexist with recurring service-period metadata without accidental coupling
Preserve negative-invoice and credit-application flows when recurring charge timing changes underneath invoice generation
Preserve credit expiration and reconciliation behavior when recurring invoice detail periods become canonical and more explicit
Ensure invoice workflow events and audit metadata remain correct when recurring charges derive from canonical service periods
Ensure duplicate-invoice prevention and billed-through calculations still work when recurring selection is driven by due service periods
Decide and implement the v1 persistence location for cadence_owner
Add cadence_owner to database schema with safe defaults and backfill for existing recurring records
Update shared, server, and package interfaces to add cadence_owner and service-period-first recurring terminology
Update repositories and models that read or write contract line billing config to surface cadence_owner and de-emphasize billing_cycle_alignment
Update server API schemas and services that create or update contract lines, templates, or billing settings to accept cadence_owner safely
Update package actions that create or update recurring contract lines, presets, templates, and wizards to persist cadence_owner
Update test utilities and fixture builders to generate service-period-first recurring scenarios across timing modes and cadence owners
Add validation rules preventing unsupported cadence-owner and timing combinations during staged rollout
Add compatibility readers and writers so existing records lacking cadence_owner continue to behave as client cadence until migrated
Prepare a staged deprecation path for billing_cycle_alignment in repositories, models, and API payloads without breaking rollout
Update recurring contract line configuration UI to present cadence owner in business terms
Update contract wizard and resume flows to capture cadence owner for newly created recurring lines
Update template detail and template wizard recurring configuration UI to capture cadence-owner defaults for template-authored lines
Update fixed recurring configuration panels to explain partial-period settlement instead of proration-as-workaround
Update billing dashboard copy, quick-start guidance, and settings descriptions to reflect service-period-first semantics
Update client billing schedule UI and preview flows to clarify that client cadence is one possible recurring service-period owner, not the only source of timing
Update client portal invoice, contract-line, and billing-detail views to display canonical service-period metadata accurately
Update reporting actions and report definitions that summarize recurring invoices or contract billing periods to interpret canonical service periods correctly
Update accounting export repositories and adapters to continue mapping stable service-period fields after cutover
Update internal docs, help text, and architecture references to describe the new recurring timing model and rollout defaults
Define monthly contract-cadence semantics anchored to assignment start date
Define quarterly contract-cadence semantics anchored to assignment start date
Define semi-annual contract-cadence semantics anchored to assignment start date
Define annual contract-cadence semantics anchored to assignment start date
Define contract-cadence behavior for future-start assignments, renew-in-place, and renewed contracts
Define first-invoice behavior for contract-cadence lines that start in the middle of an existing client billing cycle
Define final-invoice behavior for contract-cadence lines that end in the middle of a generated contract-owned period
Define invoice grouping rules when a client has both client-cadence and contract-cadence recurring lines due in the same window
Define invoice grouping rules when mixed cadence-owner recurring lines are due in different windows or at different frequencies
Update recurring billing selection and run logic to handle mixed cadence owners deterministically
Build a staged rollout plan that enables client-cadence parity before contract cadence
Add DB-backed pre-cutover validation proving existing client-cadence recurring invoices remain unchanged under the new engine
Add rollout controls or equivalent safety gates if needed to compare old and new recurring outputs before hard cutover
Document and implement migration and backfill for cadence_owner defaults on existing recurring records
Add regression guardrails to keep time, usage, materials, and manual-only flows outside the first hard cutover
Remove live execution dependence on billing_cycle_alignment once parity and contract-cadence rollout are complete
Remove obsolete recurring timing helpers, dead branches, and duplicated proration code after cutover
Add source and DB-backed post-cutover validation proving recurring timing now flows through the canonical service-period model
Add operator and developer runbooks for parity checks, mixed-cadence troubleshooting, and rollback posture
Capture follow-on plan boundaries for full time-and-usage unification once materialized recurring service periods are established in v1
Update billing settings actions and types to surface cadence-owner defaults, rollout semantics, and compatibility boundaries where recurring defaults are configured
Update billing cycle anchor actions and schedule-preview flows to coexist cleanly with explicit cadence ownership without implying that client cadence is universal
Update contract line preset actions and preset models to persist cadence-owner defaults independently from billing_cycle_alignment
Audit contract line mapping and disambiguation helpers so no recurring timing assumptions are inferred from legacy config flags after cutover
Update invoice models, services, and repository readers so full invoice detail APIs expose canonical service-period data consistently
Update invoice query and read actions used by dashboard and portal flows to preserve stable rendering semantics for canonical service periods
Update accounting export repositories and mapping schemas to consume canonical service-period metadata directly rather than legacy recurring timing assumptions
Update event schemas and billing workflow events that carry recurring invoice or billing detail so service-period-first semantics remain explicit and stable
Update contract revenue, expiration, reconciliation, and invoice-summary reporting surfaces to use canonical service-period meaning where recurring timing matters
Update client-portal billing metrics, remaining bucket unit actions, recent invoice readers, and line-detail actions to understand canonical service-period semantics
Update purchase-order service and PO-related invoice selection behavior if recurring service-period changes affect PO-scoped invoice validation
Update tax, external tax import, and tax reconciliation consumers if they rely on recurring service-period fields or charge timing assumptions
Update invoice template preview, sample invoice data, and related demo data generation so canonical service-period fields render correctly during previews
Update billing test helpers, pricing schedule helpers, and migration helpers to generate cadence-owner-aware recurring scenarios and parity fixtures
Add backfill verification that all existing recurring records receive client cadence defaults without mutating current invoice behavior
Add explicit mixed-cadence validation at action, API, and UI layers for unsupported combinations during phased rollout
Update contract line fixed-config models and services to stage billing_cycle_alignment deprecation in favor of cadence-owner and partial-period semantics
Update template-authored recurring defaults and instantiation flows so cadence-owner defaults clone safely into instantiated contract lines
Update billing dashboard overview, automatic invoices, prepayment invoices, and manual invoices tabs to explain and surface the new recurring semantics consistently
Update client-portal payment and billing overview dialogs to render canonical service periods independently of old proration assumptions
Audit company-sync, accounting mapping, and export validation services for service-period field assumptions affected by recurring timing changes
Add systematic source cleanup for obsolete recurring timing terms in UI copy, docs, and implementation comments after cutover
Add runtime guardrails preventing partial rollout states where some recurring charge families use new timing and others still use old timing on the same execution path

Preserve end-exclusive query behavior and overlap semantics in billing-engine selection logic when service-period-first timing replaces legacy helper logic
Add post-cutover source and database validation proving recurring outputs no longer depend on resolveServicePeriod or billing_cycle_alignment in live paths

Capture explicit follow-on work for advanced service-period ledger extensions such as long-range materialization, archival, or performance-oriented denormalization if v1 materialization proves insufficient
Capture explicit follow-on work for time and usage unification if future product direction requires canonical service periods beyond recurring contract-backed charges
Document an operational runbook for investigating cadence-owner disputes, service-period mismatches, and mixed-cadence invoice questions
Add a feature-to-subsystem mapping discipline in the plan artifacts so implementation progress stays traceable across runtime, invoices, APIs, UI, and downstream consumers

Define the job payload schema and selector inputs for choosing due recurring service periods in background invoice-generation flows

Define schedulable due-work selection for contract-cadence recurring obligations before they enter invoice grouping

Define invoice grouping and split rules for purchase-order constrained recurring charges when due service periods coincide

Define the operator-visible split policy and explainability metadata when one due window produces multiple invoices by design
Define a stable projection contract between invoice parent charges and canonical invoice_charge_details for recurring lines
